access.anon: On the home page, can only see the public bus and frequent links views; cannot enter the personal overview.
access.user: The home page serves as the personal workspace.
access.admin: The home page personal workspace behavior is the same as regular users.
access.agent: Can read overview results with the same semantics as the home page; REST and MCP should return consistent overview information.

rules.decision-page: The home page is not a directory page but a decision page for 'what to do next'.
rules.current-semester-priority: The above-the-fold area prioritizes personal learning tasks within the current semester, not historical semester content or complete directory information.
rules.homework-todo-layered: Homework and todos are layered as 'due today / due soon / all incomplete'; homework with no due date does not participate in upcoming due date sorting.
rules.non-current-not-disguised: Non-current-semester subscribed sections must not be disguised as current learning tasks.
rules.aggregate-first-for-assistants: Prefer a single aggregated snapshot tool for common assistant prompts before fanning out into multiple narrower tool calls.
rules.focused-extracts-share-window: Focused tools such as next-class and upcoming-deadlines should stay aligned with the same short-horizon window as the broader dashboard snapshot.

tools.name: get_my_overview
notes: overview contains pending todo, homework, today's schedule, and upcoming exam counts.
samples contains dueTodos[], dueHomeworks[], and upcomingExams[].
Count-and-sample oriented tool; use get_my_dashboard for a richer single-call assistant snapshot.
Summary mode trims samples further; optional atTime anchors today's bounds for tests or specific-day questions.

tools.name: get_my_dashboard
tools.returns: { user, currentSemester, subscriptions, nextClass, upcomingDeadlines, upcomingEvents, todos, bus }
notes: Preferred single-call snapshot for assistant workflows.
Overlaps get_my_overview but adds currentSemester, currentSemesterSections, nextClass, merged deadlines, and preferred-bus departures.
Default mode should stay assistant-compact; request full mode when the caller explicitly needs full nested schedule, room, teacher, or homework payloads.
Summary mode should be materially smaller than default, focusing on counts and the next few actionable items instead of mirroring the full compact snapshot.

tools.name: get_next_class
notes: Focused extract for 'what is my next class?' prompts.

tools.name: get_upcoming_deadlines
notes: Merges homework_due, exam, and todo_due entries into one ordered list.
Accepts optional atTime to anchor the deadline window instead of using the server clock.
